text: The surgeon identifies adhesions between the cecum and appendix within the hernia sac. Using surgical instruments, the surgeon grasps the mesoappendix and begins dividing the attachments to free the appendix from the indirect space.

The surgeon has mostly extracted the appendix from the indirect inguinal space and is carefully dissecting additional adhesions to expose the appendiceal mesentery. A window is created in the mesentery to facilitate the insertion of a ligature, allowing for the removal of the remaining meso-appendix.

The surgeon addresses residual adhesions around the appendix to create a clear base for ligation. An endo loop is placed at the base of the appendix, followed by a second loop, before proceeding to divide the appendix.
The surgeon cauterizes the exposed mucosa of the appendix stump, leaving it as it is without imbrication.
